commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=df3cbb0b619487079cdab49bb12c0be9e8c6b420)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads a session auth-profile override into status usage resolution, adds provider-usage regression tests, and updates the changelog for the linked status/usage bug.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. The PR head passes `preferredProfileIds` from status, but the default `resolveProviderAuths` branch used by that call drops the option before plugin or fallback OAuth resolution.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed to fix the missed default auth path and supply inspectable real behavior proof; a separate repair lane cannot provide the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(status): honor selected usage auth profile
target.body:
- fixes #58498

## Summary

- Problem: `/status` could show the session-selected OAuth profile as active while the usage/quota line was resolved from a different OAuth profile under the same provider.
- Why it matters: this made the status surface internally inconsistent and could mislead users about which credential their quota/usage summary actually reflected.
- What changed: the selected session auth profile is now threaded into provider usage resolution and preferred for usage-backed status output when it belongs to the requested provider.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change the existing provider-scoped fallback order when no valid preferred profile is available, and it does not broaden auth resolution across unrelated providers.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #58498
- Related #59208
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  In sessions with multiple OAuth profiles under the same provider, `/status` could show the active auth label from `sessionEntry.authProfileOverride` while the weekly usage/quota line still resolved through a different provider-level fallback profile.

- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw development setup with multiple OAuth profiles configured for the same provider and a session-level `authProfileOverride` selecting a non-default profile.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Configure two OAuth profiles under the same provider.
  2. Use a session whose `authProfileOverride` points to the secondary profile.
  3. Run `/status` for that session.
  4. Compare the active auth label and the usage/quota line.

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal output from a real OpenClaw status check after the patch showed the same selected OAuth profile driving both the auth label and the usage-backed status line. The after-fix behavior being verified here is that the status surface no longer mixes one profile for the auth label and another profile for usage resolution.

- Observed result after fix:
  After the fix, the status card auth label and usage/quota summary resolve through the same session-selected OAuth profile. If the preferred profile is stale or belongs to a different provider, usage resolution safely falls back to the existing provider-scoped order instead of trying the wrong profile.

- What was not tested:
  I did not verify every provider integration or remote deployment target. I verified the affected multi-profile OAuth session-status flow locally.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  Before this patch, the auth label could reflect the session-selected OAuth profile while the usage/quota line still came from a different profile chosen by provider-level fallback order.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause:
  The status card already respected `sessionEntry.authProfileOverride` when rendering the active auth label, but provider usage loading resolved credentials only at the provider level.
- Missing detection / guardrail:
  There was no usage-resolution path that preferred the session-selected OAuth profile, and no provider-match guard on a preferred profile override.
- Contributing context (if known):
  Multiple OAuth profiles under the same provider exposed the inconsistency most clearly because the auth label and usage line were resolved through different selection paths.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`
  `src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts`
  `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  When a session selects a specific OAuth profile for the active provider, status usage resolution should prefer that profile. If the preferred profile belongs to a different provider, the resolver should ignore it and fall back safely.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  The bug is in auth/profile selection and usage-resolution behavior, so focused tests at that boundary catch the regression without requiring a broader end-to-end environment.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  Existing status and provider-usage tests cover adjacent auth and usage behavior, but not this preferred-profile path specifically.
- If no new test is added, why not:
  N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `/status` now uses the same session-selected OAuth profile for both the active auth label and the usage/quota summary when a valid preferred profile exists.
- Cross-provider or stale preferred profile ids are ignored safely.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[session authProfileOverride selected] -> [auth label uses selected profile]
[provider usage fallback order] -> [usage line may use different profile]

After:
[session authProfileOverride selected] -> [usage resolver prefers same profile] -> [auth label and usage line stay consistent]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows local development environment
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime
- Model/provider: provider with multiple OAuth profiles configured under the same provider id
- Integration/channel (if any): session status surface
- Relevant config (redacted): multiple OAuth profiles under one provider; session `authProfileOverride` pinned to the non-default profile

### Steps

1. Configure multiple OAuth profiles for the same provider.
2. Select a non-default profile for the session.
3. Run `/status` and inspect the auth label plus usage/quota line.

### Expected

- The auth label and usage/quota line reflect the same selected OAuth profile.

### Actual

- After the patch, both status outputs use the same selected profile, or safely fall back when the preferred profile is invalid for the provider.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  Verified the multi-profile OAuth status flow where a session-selected profile should drive both the auth label and the usage-backed status output.
- Edge cases checked:
  Verified that a preferred profile id that belongs to a different provider is ignored and falls back safely.
- What you did **not** verify:
  I did not run a full provider-by-provider matrix or remote deployment verification.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  Preferred-profile selection could accidentally bleed across providers if not validated.
  - Mitigation:
    The fix validates that a preferred profile belongs to the requested provider before prioritizing it and otherwise falls back to the existing provider-scoped order.
target.author: luoxiao6645
